request: design a simple fluid network for a rocket that uses a pressure fed system with gaseous nitrogen as the pressurant, lox as the oxidizer, and kerosene as the fuel.

requirements.name: pressure_fed_rocket_feed_system_1b344675
requirements.description: Design a simple pressure-fed rocket feed system using gaseous nitrogen (GN2) as the pressurant, liquid oxygen (LOX) as the oxidizer, and kerosene (RP-1) as the fuel. The system must include a GN2 pressurant tank, LOX tank, kerosene tank, and appropriate flow connections to deliver propellants to the engine. FIXED quantities: propellant types (GN2, LOX, kerosene). TUNABLE quantities: tank volumes, initial pressures, flow rates, pipe diameters, and component sizing. The design must achieve stable propellant delivery with non-zero mass flow to the engine.

notes: Use appropriate fluid properties for GN2 (gas), LOX (cryogenic liquid), and kerosene (RP-1)
Ensure pressurization lines maintain tank pressure above ullage pressure
Design feed lines to provide sufficient mass flow for engine operation
Consider pressure drop across feed lines and valves
